bunny.title: Bunny
bunny.hudKing: King of the Hill — Hold the Zone!
bunny.tutorialWelcomeDesc: A ragdoll physics game where you control a bunny. Roll around, jump, and fight other bunnies!
bunny.tutorialMovementDesc: Use Left/Right (keyboard arrows or gamepad stick) to roll your bunny along the ground.
bunny.tutorialStandDesc: Press A (gamepad) or Space to switch from doggy (all fours) to plank (upright). While standing, press again to jump!
bunny.tutorialAttackDesc: The key to combat! Stand up (plank pose), then spin toward your opponent. Your extended legs deal damage on impact. Get the angle and distance right!
bunny.tutorialAttackTip: Tip: Roll toward an enemy, stand up at the right moment, and let your legs swing into them. High-velocity leg contact is lethal!
bunny.tutorialGrabDesc: Hold LB (gamepad) or Shift to grab platforms or other bunnies. Use this to anchor yourself or throw opponents!
bunny.tutorialModesDesc: Arena: Last bunny standing wins. Puzzle: Collect all coins. Sumo: Push enemies off the platform. Race: Reach the finish first. King: Hold the hill zone!
bunny.tooltipPose: Toggle between doggy (all fours) and plank (standing). Standing lets you kick!
bunny.tooltipRoll: Roll to move. Momentum builds up — use it to slam into enemies.
bunny.tooltipGrab: Grab nearby surfaces or enemies. Break free by building speed.
bunny.tooltipKick: While standing, your legs can knock enemies into spikes or off the map!

empire.title: Empire RTS
empire.description: Kingdom-style side-scrolling RTS — build your empire, recruit units & defend against waves.
empire.campaignDesc: Progress through chapters and conquer the land
empire.ch1Desc: Establish your castle and survive the first nights.
empire.ch2Desc: Build a barracks and train warriors to push the frontier.
empire.ch3Desc: Survive 10 waves of increasingly brutal enemies.
empire.ch4Desc: Build a full economy with mines, farms and lumber mills.
empire.ch5Desc: Face the boss horde and protect your castle at all costs.
empire.tutorialWelcomeDesc: A Kingdom Two Crowns-style strategy game. Manage your economy, build defences and survive enemy waves.
empire.tutorialEconomyDesc: Collect gold coins and deposit them at buildings. Your pouch has limited capacity — build a Treasury to expand it.
empire.tutorialBuildingDesc: Cycle through buildings with Q/Tab (or LB/RB) and place with F (or Y). Buildings need gold and wood.
empire.tutorialCombatDesc: Recruit warriors, archers and monks from their buildings. They fight enemies automatically.
empire.tutorialWavesDesc: Enemies attack in waves, growing stronger each night. Build towers and train troops to survive.

magicDecks.menuSubtitle: Build decks, battle opponents, collect cards
magicDecks.tutorialDesc: Learn the basics step by step

campaign.title: ⚔️ Campaign
campaign.subtitle: Battle through chapters to earn cards and XP
campaign.skirmishDesc: Fight a random opponent at your level. Capture cards by defeating them decisively!

magicDecks.deckBuilderDesc: Build and rate your custom decks
magicDecks.skirmishDesc2: Find a random opponent near your level
magicDecks.tutorialWelcomeDesc: This tutorial will teach you the basics of card battling. Follow along step by step!
magicDecks.tutorialHandDesc: These are your cards. Use A/D or ←/→ to browse them. Each card has attack, defense, mana cost and element.
magicDecks.tutorialManaDesc: Mana regenerates over time. You need mana to play cards. Watch the mana bar — plan your plays!
magicDecks.tutorialPlayDesc: Select a card and press Space or Enter to play it. It will appear on the battlefield in your selected lane.
magicDecks.tutorialLanesDesc: The battlefield has 5 lanes. Use W/S or ↑/↓ to select a lane. Creatures attack straight ahead.
magicDecks.tutorialCombatDesc: Creatures attack automatically when ready. They deal damage based on their attack stat. Defense reduces incoming damage.
magicDecks.tutorialElementsDesc: There are 6 elements: Fire, Water, Earth, Air, Light, Dark. Each has advantages — Fire beats Earth, Water beats Fire, etc.
magicDecks.tutorialEvolveDesc: Some creatures can evolve into stronger forms! Press E or / when a creature is ready. Evolution costs 2 extra mana.
magicDecks.tutorialSpellsDesc: Spell cards have instant effects — heal, damage, buff, or debuff. They don't stay on the field.
magicDecks.tutorialAbilitiesDesc: Creatures have passive and active abilities. Passives trigger automatically, actives happen on play or attack.
magicDecks.tutorialWinDesc: Reduce your opponent's life to 0 to win! Unblocked creatures deal direct damage to the opponent.
magicDecks.tutorialGoodLuckDesc: You know the basics now. Go win this tutorial battle! Remember: element advantages, evolution, and mana management are key.
magicDecks.hintFirstTurn: Play a low-cost card to establish board presence early!
magicDecks.hintLowMana: Low on mana — wait for it to regenerate before playing expensive cards.
magicDecks.hintFullHand: Your hand is full! Play cards before you draw more or you'll waste them.
magicDecks.hintEmptyField: Your field is empty! Play a creature to defend your life points.
magicDecks.hintCanEvolve: A creature is ready to evolve! Press E or / to make it stronger.
magicDecks.hintElementAdvantage: You have an element advantage in a lane — press the attack!
magicDecks.hintLowLife: Your life is low! Consider defensive plays or healing spells.
magicDecks.hintComboChance: Multiple creatures of the same element — abilities may combo!
magicDecks.hintSpellAvailable: You have a spell card — use it for a tactical advantage!
magicDecks.hintBossWeak: The boss is weakening! Keep up the pressure!
magicDecks.tipAddMoreSameElement: Add more cards of the same element for better synergy.
magicDecks.tipAddEvolutions: Include evolution chains for powerful mid-game plays.
magicDecks.tipBalanceCost: Balance your mana costs — too many expensive cards is risky.
magicDecks.tipAddLowCost: Add more low-cost cards for early game presence.
magicDecks.tipAddVariety: Include different card types for more tactical options.
magicDecks.tipMoreCreatures: Add more creatures — you need board presence to win.
magicDecks.tipStrongerCards: Consider adding higher-stat creatures for better trading.

menu.title: 🃏 Magic Decks TCG

vocalReport.stdDevDesc: Pitch consistency; lower = more stable intonation
vocalReport.intonationAccuracyDesc: Percentage of points within ±50 cents of target
vocalReport.perfectHitRateDesc: Percentage of points within ±15 cents (near-perfect intonation)
vocalReport.sungVsExpected: Sung pitch (blue) vs. Expected pitch (green) — MIDI note numbers
vocalReport.preWindowDesc: Time before note start to accept pitch
vocalReport.totalNotesDesc: Number of note descriptors in the song
vocalReport.methDataAcqText: Audio input was captured via the Web Audio API using the configured microphone device. The raw audio stream was fed into the selected pitch detection algorithm ({{algorithm}}) which extracted fundamental frequency (F0) estimates at ~60 Hz sampling rate.
vocalReport.methPitchAccText: Sung pitch (Hz) was converted to MIDI note numbers via MIDI = 12 × log₂(f/440) + 69. Deviation from the expected note was computed in cents (1 semitone = 100 cents). Points within ±50¢ are classified as "on-pitch"; within ±15¢ as "perfect".
vocalReport.methStatAnalysisText: Mean absolute deviation (MAD), median, and standard deviation (σ) were computed across all voiced frames within note regions. The deviation histogram shows the distribution of cent errors in 10 bins, enabling visual identification of systematic pitch bias.
vocalReport.methVibratoText: Oscillation patterns were identified using zero-crossing analysis on detrended pitch contours within 500ms sliding windows. Vibrato was classified as present when the oscillation rate fell within 3–12 Hz (physiological vibrato range, per Sundberg 1987).
vocalReport.methJitterText: Jitter (F0 perturbation) was computed as the average cycle-to-cycle variation in MIDI note values, normalized to percentage. Vocal presence (shimmer proxy) represents the ratio of voiced to total frames — a measure of signal quality.
vocalReport.methGradingText: The overall grade is a weighted composite: Intonation Accuracy (30%), Perfect Hit Rate (20%), Rhythm Stability (15%), Total Score (15%), Vocal Presence (10%), Combo Rate (10%). Letter grades follow the standard academic scale (A+ ≥ 95%, F < 40%).
vocalReport.methReferencesText: Sundberg, J. (1987). The Science of the Singing Voice. DeKalb: Northern Illinois University Press. | Boersma, P. (1993). "Accurate short-term analysis of the fundamental period and the harmonics-to-noise ratio of a sampled sound." IFA Proceedings, 17, pp. 97–110.
vocalReport.conclusionAchieved: The performer {{player}} achieved a total score of {{score}} points (out of 10,000) on the song {{song}} at {{difficulty}} difficulty.
vocalReport.conclusionAnalysis: Pitch analysis based on {{samples}} audio samples reveals:
vocalReport.conclusionIntonation: Intonation accuracy of {{pct}}% with {{perfectPct}}% perfect hits
vocalReport.conclusionDeviation: Mean absolute pitch deviation: {{cents}} cents (σ = {{std}} cents)
vocalReport.conclusionCombo: Maximum combo streak of {{combo}}× consecutive note hits
vocalReport.conclusionVibrato: Natural vibrato detected at {{rate}} Hz, ±{{extent}} cents extent
vocalReport.footerGenerated: Generated by AudioVerse Vocal Performance Analyzer v2.0
vocalReport.footerCopyright: All measurements computed client-side using Web Audio API. No audio data was transmitted to external servers.
vocalReport.gradeAPlus: Exceptional vocal performance — professional-grade intonation and rhythm
vocalReport.gradeA: Excellent — near-perfect pitch accuracy with strong consistency
vocalReport.gradeAMinus: Very good — minor deviations but overall outstanding
vocalReport.gradeBPlus: Good — solid pitch control with occasional inaccuracies
vocalReport.gradeB: Above average — generally accurate with some areas for improvement
vocalReport.gradeBMinus: Decent — noticeable pitch deviations in several sections
vocalReport.gradeCPlus: Average — adequate performance with room for improvement
vocalReport.gradeC: Below average — frequent pitch deviations noted
vocalReport.gradeCMinus: Developing — significant areas for improvement identified
vocalReport.gradeDPlus: Weak — considerable pitch and rhythm challenges observed
vocalReport.gradeD: Poor — major difficulties with pitch accuracy and timing
vocalReport.gradeDMinus: Very poor — fundamental vocal technique improvement needed
vocalReport.gradeF: Insufficient — unable to maintain pitch accuracy at this difficulty level
